title: Offload to Web Worker
description:
Offload an expensive computation (sorting + statistical analysis of large datasets) to a Web Worker to keep the main thread responsive.

### Requirements

- Create a Web Worker that accepts a dataset and returns sorted results + statistics
- The main thread posts data to the worker and receives results via `onmessage`
- Show a processing indicator while the worker is computing
- The UI (e.g., a counter button) must remain responsive during computation
- Terminate the worker on component unmount
- Handle worker errors gracefully with an error state
- A "Process" button triggers the computation; results are displayed after

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Process button | `process-btn` |
| Counter button | `counter-btn` |
| Counter display | `counter-value` |
| Processing indicator | `processing` |
| Result stats | `result-stats` |
| Error display | `error-display` |
| Status text | `status` |
constraints:
- Must use a real Web Worker (inline worker via Blob URL if needed)
- The main thread must stay responsive during computation
- Worker must be terminated on unmount
- Must handle worker errors
- Counter must be clickable and responsive during processing
hints: Create an inline worker with `new Blob([workerCode], { type: 'application/javascript' })` and `URL.createObjectURL`.
Inside the worker: listen for 'message', do the heavy computation, then postMessage the results back.
Use useRef to store the worker instance. In the cleanup function of useEffect, call worker.terminate().
